original: La Soufrière volcano begins erupting on the Caribbean island of St. Vincent with a plume of ash 20,000 feet into the sky
NASA names 1st 7 astronauts for Project Mercury
Wolves have returned to the Netherlands after 140 years claim ecologists
First recorded sighting in 1910 of Halley's Comet, seen in Gore, New Zealand
J. Presper Eckert, Jr., American engineer and co-inventor (1st electronic computer-ENIAC), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1995)
Eadweard Muybridge, English photographer, pioneer in study of motion, born in Kingston upon Thames, England (d. 1904)
Colorado Rockies 1st home game & 1st victory, 11-4 over Mont Expos
World's largest bunny hop at Radio City Music Hall, New York City; record eclipsed in 2007
1st flight of Concorde 002 (Filton-Bristol)
Jørn Utzon, Danish architect (Sydney Opera House), born in Copenhagen, Denmark (d. 2008)
Gregory Pincus, American biologist and inventor (birth control pill), born in Woodbine, New Jersey (d. 1903)
Kuwaiti media outlet, Kuwaiti News unveils virtual news presenter on Twitter speaking Arabic, using artificial intelligence (although China was the 1st in 2018)
1st baseball game at Houston Astrodome, Astros beat NY Yankees 2-1 in exhibition asMickey Mantlehits MLB's 1st indoor homerun; US President Lyndon Johnson and wife Lady Bird attend
Los Angeles DodgersFernando Valenzuelamakes MLB debut as opening day starter due to pitching staff injuries, beats Astros 2-0; goes on to win both Rookie of the Year and Cy Young Awards

American contraltoMarian Andersonsings before 75,000 at the Lincoln Memorial in Washington, D.C; appearance organized after Anderson was denied permission to sing to an integrated audience in Constitution Hall

"Paper Moon" film starring Ryan O'Neal and his daughter, Tatum O'Neal, directed byPeter Bogdanovichpremieres in Hollywood, California; Tatum O'Neal becomes youngest (age 10) Academy Award winner (Supporting Actress)

Edward V, aged 12, succeeds his fatherEdward IVas King of England. He is never crowned and disappears, presumed murdered, after being incarcerated in the Tower of London with his younger brother Richard (the "Princes in the Tower")
At the height of his fameBabe Ruthrushed to hospital with a mysterious condition labelled “the bellyache heard round the world". Ruth reported dead by some newspapers.
In separate NBA games Denver's David Thompson scores 73 points & San Antonio'sGeorge Gervinscores 63 points (33 in 1 quarter)
For 3rd time,Wayne Gretzky, scores 7 points (1 goal, 6 assists) in a Stanley Cup game and passes Jean Béliveau as all time playoff scoring champ
LA Lakers block 21 Denver shots setting NBA regulation game record
Brewers sweep Orioles 11-3, 16-3, & 13-5 (each with a grand slam)
1st exhibition baseball game at Fenway Park (Red Sox vs Harvard)
San Diego Padres pitcher Joe Musgrove no-hits the Texas Rangers, 3-0 at Globe Life Field, Arlington, TX
6th NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Challenger 1 returns to Earth
STS-59 (Endeavour) launches into orbit
Soyuz 35 carries 2 cosmonauts to Salyut 6
Jim Fowler, American naturalist, zoologist and Emmy Award-winning TV presenter (Mutual of Omaha's Wild Kingdom), born in Albany, Georgia (d. 2019)
Charles Proteus Steinmetz, German-American electrical engineer (development of alternating current), born in Breslau, Province of Silesia, Prussia (d. 1923)
Martin Luther King Jr., buried in Atlanta
US SenatorTammy Duckworthof Illinois becomes the first senator to give birth while in office
A group of older Swiss women win a landmark climate change case at the European Court of Human Rights, ruling the women's rights had been violated by not fully addressing climate change
Confederate GeneralRobert E. Leeand 26,765 troops surrender at Appomattox Court House to U.S. Lieutenant General Ulysses S. Grant, ending the Civil War in Northern Virginia
Vimy Ridgein France stormed by Canadian troops
Winston Churchillbecomes 1st honorary US citizen
David Bowie's single "Let's Dance" becomes his 1st song to hit #1 in both the US and the UK
"The Lion King" becomes highest grossing Broadway show, overtaking "The Phantom of the Opera"
Tom Lehrer, American musician ("The Elements"), satirist (That Was The Week That Was), and mathematician, born in New York City (d. 2025)[1][2]
Marty Krofft, Canadian puppeteer and television producer - with his brother Sid (H.R. Pufnstuf; Land of the Lost; Sigmund and the Sea Monsters; Donny & Marie), born in Montreal, Quebec (d. 2023)[1]
Beatles "Ticket to Ride" single is released in UK
Ringo Starrreleases single "It Don't Come Easy", produced byGeorge Harrison, in the UK

Rock for the Rainforest benefit concert held at Carnegie Hall, NYC; performers include:Sting,Elton John,James Taylor,Whitney Houston,Luciano Pavarotti, Aaron Neville,Tammy Wynette, Branford Marsalis, and Larry Adler
Funeral ofQueen Elizabeth, the Queen Motherat Westminster Abbey UK. More than a million people line the streets
Mockumentary "Parks and Recreation" starringAmy Poehler, Nick Offerman andAziz Ansaridebuts on NBC in the US
13th NBA Championship: Boston Celtics sweep Minneapolis Lakers in 4 games, first of Boston Celtics' record 8 straight titles
Minn's Wayne Connelly is 1st to score on a Stanley Cup penalty shot
Stuart Parkin is awarded the Millennium Technology Prize for his work on magnetic storage
Civil Rights Bill passes over PresidentAndrew Johnson's veto
John Hancockrefuses to allow two British customs agents to go below deck of his ship, considered by some to be the first act of physical resistance to British authority in the colonies
US & Russia agreed on size of nuclear tests for peaceful use
Theobald Boehm, German inventor of the modern flute, born in Munich, Electorate of Bavaria, Holy Roman Empire (d. 1881)
Sibrandus Stratingh, Dutch physician and chemist (electric car), born in Adorp, Netherlands (d. 1841)
Kenneth Cockrell, American astronaut (STS 56, 69, 80), born in Austin, Texas
Steve Gadd, American jazz and rock session drummer (Paul Simon - "Late In The Evening"; "50 Ways To Leave Your Lover"; Steely Dan - "Aja"'), born in Irondequoit, New York
Victor Vasarely, Hungarian-French painter and author (Op Art), born in Pécs, Hungary, Austro-Hungarian Empire (d. 1997)
Robert Helpmann, Australian actor, choreographer and dancer (Chitty Chitty Bang Bang, Don Quixote), born in Mount Gambier, South Australia (d. 1986)
Frankie Thomas, American actor and author (Tom Corbett Space Cadet), born in New York City (d. 2006)

Rockin' Sidney [Simien], American zydeco and R&B singer-songwriter, accordionist, guitarist, organist, and harmonica player ("My Toot-Toot"; "You Ain't Nothing But Fine"), born in Lebeau, Louisiana (d. 1998)
Geoff Palmer [Godfrey Palmer], Jamaican-British grain scientist, activist and the first Black professor in Scotland, born in St Elizabeth, Jamaica (d. 2025)
Jimmy Roberts, Canadian ice hockey defenceman (NHL All Star 1965, 69, 70; Stanley Cup 1965, 66, 73, 76, 77; Montreal Canadiens), born in Toronto (d. 2015)
